blocks.content: November. Meilin knew something was different about Eli the moment he suggested they take a walk.

blocks.speaker: Meilin
blocks.content: You hate walking.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I appreciate walking. I've always appreciated walking.

blocks.speaker: Meilin
blocks.content: You complained about walking to the mailbox last week.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: That's a different kind of walking.
She went with him anyway. Millhaven on a November evening — the last of the leaves down, the air cold and clean, the houses beginning to put lights in their windows.

blocks.speaker: Meilin
blocks.content: Where are we going.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Nowhere specific.

blocks.speaker: Meilin
blocks.content: Eli.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I just wanted to walk with you.
She looked at him. He was looking straight ahead, hands in his pockets, the tips of his ears red in the cold.

blocks.speaker: Meilin
blocks.content: Are you nervous.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I'm not nervous.

blocks.speaker: Meilin
blocks.content: Your ears are red.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: It's cold.

blocks.speaker: Meilin
blocks.content: Eli.
He stopped walking. They were at the corner of a street.
He turned to her. Reached into his jacket pocket.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I don't have a film. I don't have a field or a plan or lanterns. I'm not Kit.

blocks.speaker: Meilin
blocks.content: I know you're not Kit.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: What I have is — you. And the absolute certainty that I don't want to do anything that's coming next without you in it.
He held up the ring. Simple, small, exactly right.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Meilin Bai???????????
blocks.translation: Meilin Bai. Will you marry me. Please.

blocks.content: She stared at him. He had been practicing this for three weeks — in secret, from recordings, getting the tones wrong and starting over.
The please made her laugh. The ring made her cry. She said yes.
